entry.title: SQL OEM Licence by SAP
entry.content:
We are reaching out on behalf of our customer,who holds a SQL OEM license supplied by SAP. They have a few queries related to their licensing needs and we would appreciate your guidance on the following:

  1. Provision for Failover Replication
    Customer is planning to implement failover replication for their SQL database and would like to know if it is possible to obtain a copy of the license to be installed on both the live and replication servers. Could SAP provide an additional license copy for this purpose, or would they need to purchase a new license?
  2. License Binding to Hardware or Virtual Machines
    We need clarification on whether the SQL OEM license supplied by SAP is locked to specific hardware or the virtual machine (VM). If the customer moves their VM from one host to another, will the existing license key remain valid?
  3. Non-SAP Staging Database Usage
    Is it permissible to host a non-SAP staging database on the same SQL Server that is OEM licensed by SAP?

 Please let us know if any additional information or documentation is needed to assist with the evaluation.

 

Looking forward to your response

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA 1909 DSD Vehicle data tab in Equipment master
entry.content:
Hello,

We have the same issue, dsd is already activate in the system, what else can we do

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow Blog Series Part 7 – Performance
entry.content:
Hello @DanielIngenhaag ,

I have a question:

if the same record (identified by the key) is updated between 2 delta data extractions,

And we replicate the data through CDS View,

and we have set some level of parallelism in replication flow.

Can we have inconsistent data due to multithread collisions?

In source system multiple packages are created in RDB, if the same record (identified by the key) is captured from source in different moment and put in different packages, these packages are loaded in parallel from RDB to Datasphere local tables:

is there a possibility that the version of the record written to Datasphere is not the last one? or there are mechanism to avoid this?

Best Regards,
Alberto

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit : Migrate your Data using Staging Tables a CO-Cost Center Object case!
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA 2020 and later versions, you need to use Migrate Your Data Fiori App instead of LTMC, you can still use LTMC in S4Hana 2020 but those projects will not be changed using Migrate Your Data app you can only display it.

In this document we will explain how to use the new migration application (Migrate your data app).

 

Scope of the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit :

The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit is designed for customers who have just installed SAP S/4HANA or are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud and want to move their legacy data from SAP or non-SAP software systems. It allows users to migrate their master data and transactional data to SAP S/4HANA, and it facilitates this process by providing predefined migration content and mapping. The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit is part of both SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit and associated content (migration objects) are neither intended nor recommended to be used as an interface to frequently load data into a system or to mass change existing data. For more details, see SAP Note 2684818 - migration cockpit usage.

Main functions and features of the application :

In SAP S/4HANA 2020 release, the approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" has the following functionality:

  1. Select and extract data in source system via RFC.
  2. Transfer data from SAP source system to target SAP S/4HANA system.

Data Migration Steps

The process of data migration follows a sequence of six steps. We will see in detail each of these steps: 

Prerequisite – Business role: SAP_BR_CONFIG_EXPERT_DATA_MIG Search for the app Migrate your Data in Fiori:

Step 1: On Migrate Your Data app create and Select Migrate Data Using Staging Tables:

Step 2: Here open in your browser, you may: Insert a name / Select a Mass Transfer ID / Select the option Local SAP S/4HANA Database Schema in the Database Connection ⏩Go to Step 2

Step 3: In this step you may Select which Migration Objects you want to migrate, for this just Select the box on the left side of the name of the object and click on the button Select:

Step 4: The selected objects will appear on the right side in the Selected Migration Object section. I just selected one object (CO-Cost Center), but you can select as many objects as you need.

Step 5: This page will appear to confirm the information of your project and its objects, if you don’t want to change anything, go to Create Project

Step 6: After creating the project, you will be directed to the main page of the Migrate Your Data app and you will be able to see the project you have created, in this case, the name I assigned is ZCOST_CENTER.

Step 7: Once you open the project, the object’s info will be displayed. Click on the Download Template option to get the XML file that must be filled:

Step 8: Once you downloaded the XML file, open it with Ms Excel and fill all the fields with the data you want to migrate.

Step 9: After filling and saving your XML file, go to Update File:

Step 10: This page will be displayed, click on Upload to Select the XML file:

Step 11: After you select the file, it will be displayed as below. When the status change to Data Successfully Transferred to Staging Tables, click to go back on the top:

Step 12: Select the Prepare button to start the process:

Step 13: The status will be updated automatically, left-click on the option Mapping Tasks:

Step 14: In this step, you will be required to confirm the values of the fields you inserted in the XML file.

  1. You can select all the tasks on the left side and click on the Confirm to complete them at one time. •
  2. Another option is to click on each one and confirm the values.

Step 15: After all confirmation statuses are confirmed, click to go back:

Step 16:The Simulation step will be ready, click to start:

Step 17: Once the simulation status is updated to Successful, click on Migrate to start the migration:

Confirmation: A confirmation will be required, click on Ok!

Step 18: The % of the Migration Progress will be updated in the progress bar, once it gets 100% your data will be available in the system tables:

Your data is now available in the SAP S/4HANA tables:

Data uploaded into S/4HANA table

entry.title: SAP CodeJam 2025 in Krakow, Poland
entry.content:
Hi SAP CodeJam Team,

we would like to host a SAP CodeJam in April this year.

  • Krakow, Poland

  • April 3, 2025. 10 a.m. - 4 p.m.

If you need further information please contact me natallia_shtura@epam.com

Thanks in advance!

Natallia Shtura

entry.title: Chemical Vs. Material in EHS S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 
I have a question about EHS in SAP S/4HANA. In this area, we have to choose between Chemical and Material as the note below describes. 

Our client needs Risk Assessment for Chemicals, so we need The Chemical Solution. also, the client needs Waste Management, so we need The Material Solution. How can manage both of them?
links: 
Chemical 
Materials 

Regrads

entry.title: Re: Learning RAP100 tutorials with permission related issues
entry.content: I also met the same error in the tutorials, need help too.

entry.title: Polluted by many questions NOT about SAP Websites in Community 2024
entry.content:
There are two big issues in the Q&A of Community 2024: people using the button "Answer" (means "give solution") to just comment without giving a solution (the issue was addressed, let's see if that improves), and the other one is:

Half of my inbox is polluted by people asking a non-SAP-Websites question in the group "Question about SAP Websites".

The reason is clear for me, they don't know where to post their SAP question, even if they search, they don't find the right group:

  • The top menu contains "Products and Technology > Questions about SAP Websites", they choose it because they don't see a group corresponding to their question and because it contains the word "SAP" (lazy solution of course).
  • The top level "Create a post" is missing a search field, so they can't find easily, and they choose the easiest choice "Questions about SAP Websites".

     

  • Even if a search field existed, make sure to extend the search to the group description, e.g. there's no group "sap business one", the right group is ERP Q&A ("SAP Business One" is in the group description):

The six first months, I did tell the Original Poster that the question was posted to the wrong group, then I decided to alert the moderator to move the question to the right group.

No idea what's the best solution.

Is that in the list of planned enhancements? If not, could you add it?

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: API extract all departments including head / manager of department
entry.content:
Hi 

This may be impossible, but we export all departments with legal entity from Employee Central via API to build organization hierarchy outside of SAP, but there's no easy way to also retrieve managers of any departments?

I haven't found any link between department and manager for employees in the department.

neither an easy way to export a manager and all connected employees through API

entry.title: Modify the material quantity or price in a posted purchase order
entry.content:
In the SAP MM module, after a purchase order has been posted, how can I modify the material quantity or price in the already posted purchase order?

I am currently learning the MM (Material Management) module in SAP ERP, specifically the procurement process. I have already understood the basic process of creating purchase orders. Today, while posting a purchase order, I was wondering how to modify an already posted purchase order if there is an error in its content. I am using SAP Logon 750. I have read the relevant training materials and official documentation, but I am still unclear on some details. I would appreciate some specific guidance or best practice recommendations. Any related screenshots or step-by-step instructions would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your help。

entry.title: Re: GST Reconciliation Report - India
entry.content:
Hi,

The "India withholding tax TDS status report" app can be used to view withholding tax line items for both supplier and customer documents for a specific reporting period.

For more information related to the India localization you can refer to the below help document,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/1e382c324ae9407eaea24f48de973fb2.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

Atanu

entry.title: Re: Multilingual content in SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content: Thanks for pointing me in the right direction Chris. Sorry to do this, but can you point out where one would change the setting such that they only need to complete one of the objects (obviously their chosen language one). Cannot seem to find that setting.

entry.title: Re: Canada ECP- Cross-year retro payroll and tax for Cana...
entry.content:
I am not expert in Can payroll, then i cannot comment how to handle for tax 😄

 

However i believe we can still follow the same approach for standard SAP by following these steps:

(1) Import the Old result (the original payroll), for this case is PP26/2024 to get the amount already computed and paid is 4k.

(2) Compare the current one (for PP26/2024 in PP01/2025), then now only 2.4

(3) Generate the difference as 1.6k

(4) with LRT solution, then can bring this 1.6k to current period (for PP01/2025 in PP01/2025).

(5) manipulate to generate IT with a WT that impact to Tax with value as 1.6k. Then tax in PP01/2025 should be reduced accordingly. (same way like HR to update -amt under Infotype directly, now we use PY schema to generate it automatically.

 

Some of documents i have written long time back you can refer.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/understanding-retroactive-accounting/ba-p/13229585

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/payroll-processing-calculate-average-6-month-basic-salary/ba-p/13240434

For step 1 -> 4 above, for sure can implement. 

If you want you can refer the standard SAP payroll for Singapore when emp has backdated changed related to CPF (1 type of payment to goverment monthy), this difference in backdated must carry to current month to compute CPF, instead re-compute in retro month.

This sub schema RDF1 is handling this part.

 

If you have any further issues or having difficulties, let me know i can help.

Best regards,

Woody Nguyen

entry.title: Error SYST: Date 01.01.0000 not expected when executing FIAAIN_PO_TRACK
entry.content:
Dear All,

We are setting up India in our S4 system and when we check if report FIAAIN_PO_TRACK works with our data, we get error message no. FGV004 'SYST: Date 01.01.0000 not expected'.

I've read a lot of documents on community page as well as on SAP portal but cannot see a reason why this would show.

Things I have already checked:

1. Asset master has capitalisation date saved.

2. Asset master has depreciation date saved.

3. Asset master put to use date (I get the error whether its populated or not).

4. I checked my own user profile settings for date format.

5. The transfer date parameters in SPRO are populated and saved.

Does anyone have any suggestions as to what else might be producting this error?

Many Thanks

entry.title: Re: I want to monitor my SQLA database with Grafana monit...
entry.content: 1-Can you please tell me which method? Grafana only has a plugin for ODBC database (SQLyxe data source), unfortunately it does not support cloud.

entry.title: GROW with SAP, Resource 1 Pager (Base & Premium)
entry.content:
This is a short blog post - and that's the point.

In my previous blogs, we delved into the components of the GROW with SAP, base edition, and the GROW with SAP, premium edition bundles  - but these are long reads.

For your convenience, the adoption office has created the resource pictured and linked in the image below with key information and resources for each entitlement.

Link to document

Click the image to download

Note that some resources, coloured in grey, are not yet available - the document will be updated if/when they are made so.

If you have any additional/alternate resources to recommend, please suggest them in the comments section!

entry.title: How to share a Saved Search with another user in SAP CRM
entry.content:
When you create a Saved Search in SAP CRM and you want to share it with your colleagues or you are an SAP or you are the SAP IT guy in your company and someone from the sales department comes to you asking to copy over their coworkers Saved Search, here is a quick tutorial on who to:

  1.  

    Starting point is your root user who has a saved search they want to copy over to another user

    while the target user has no entries
  2. The user who wants to share their work has to click on the figure in the top right corner and select Share
  3. In the following dialog you click the Add button in the Shared Items section in the Central Sharing Tool and select Saved Search:
  4.  Search for the Saved Searches you want to share, select them and click on Choose:


  5. Click on the Add button in the Share With section in the Central Share Tool and select User (or one of the other options if you want)


  6. Search for the user and click on Choose:

  7. As the second to last step you click on Share:

    and wait for the confirmation to appear
  8. And as the final step switch to the target user, reload the page and verify all the selected Saved Searches are there.

Congratulations! Well done!

entry.title: How to skip/ignore rule on workflow approval
entry.content:
Hi,
So we've set up a custom MDF to "sign" contracts.
In short, this is an object that a manager can create on an employee, and the employee will approve this workflow. It consists of several fields, one of which is "Valid to date".

The employee should be able to change the "Valid to date" on the object, but only between "today()" and "Original.ToDate". Meaning the employee should be able to initiate ending of their contract. (Might be a better way all together of "ending the contract" all together? Without deleting the record, as we need the history of the contract)

As of now, we've set a simple validation business rule on this. It will raise an error message if ToDate is before Today() or after original.ToDate, AND if the LoginUser equals the user of the object.

This almost works. A user is prevented from changing the date outside of these dates. And a manager is able to change outside these dates. But when a manager does this, to for example extend the contract by changing the "ToDate" field to a later date, the contact goes on a new approval workflow (as it should) to the employee. But when the employee wants to approve this, they face the same Error message as above.

Is there a way around this? To allow a user to approve a change initiated from a manager, that would otherwise ble disallowed if it was initiated from themselves?

I'm open for any inputs on this logic. Maybe it would be more correct to allow changes in the future for end user also, but put all changes on a workflow to the manger to approve. I just thought the user should be able to end their contract without approval (in this particular usecase). 
Would still be interested to know if there is a way to set up the business rules to take context of workflows into consideration.

One possible, but not optimal way, could be to have an invisible field populated when a change is initiated by a manager, that would be part of the rule. if initiated by manager, do not show error.

entry.title: Re: Access Side Panel Content Via Fiori Launchpad

entry.title: Re: Movement type - Blocked stock - Sales order stock to...
entry.content: use the movement type 419

entry.title: Cannot Execute Adjustment Confirmation
entry.content:
Undoing the release was successful, and the first attempt at an Adjustment Confirmation of the Warehouse Order was successful, but the team was unable to Adjust the remaining Load lines of the Warehouse Order due to a “Action Failed -----> Serial number A1-97879 cannot be deleted; this is not the last active document” error.

Unfortunately they do not see any active document later than this and therefore cannot move forward. Sales Order 1343 Document Flow Extended View shows multiple Warehouse Confirmations that were successful. Outbound Delivery 956 Document Flow shows that all the Goods and Activity Confirmations have been Reversed.

entry.title: Re: New Installation of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 – Part 2 – Installation

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Digital Manufacturing with Microsoft Teams: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Hello Mr. Singhal,
thank you for this great blog post!
Would it be possible for you to either update this blog post or create a new one which includes MS workflows instead of MS webhooks? Seems like this is already retired: Retirement of Office 365 connectors within Microsoft Teams We would highly appreciate this! Thank you!
Best regards,
Simon

entry.title: What's the best practice to update story reports to new Performance Form Templates/Goal Plans YOY?
entry.content:
Hi, we're new to Story Reports and just started building our own Performance Dashboards from scratch (did not see any store templates out there yet). Our business launches 4+ different Performance Form Templates throughout the year, and then duplicates/updated of those form templates year over year. I will need to duplicate and update the dashboards for each of these processes year over year, what is the best practice to do this without needing to rebuild the dashboards from scratch each process? I figure it is best to ask now while the dashboards are still simple and I can adjust our way of building as needed, but the planned metrics we are looking to build will soon make them very complex.

Some additional information:

  • We do not have Employee Central
  • Typically use:
    • Performance Management
    • User Information
    • Employee Profile
    • Performance Goals
    • Succession Nominations
    • Position information
  • Will build 1-3 Data Sources per dashboard (so far), but anticipate this increasing

Example Query Designer Data source (again, currently simple and re-buildable, but looking for best practice not to have to do this as the dashboard evolves into more complex): 

Thank you in advance for any feedback you have!

Geena

entry.title: Re: Bring Open-Source LLMs into SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Thanks YatseaLi for the informative post

Could you provide details or reference links/blogs to integrate the SAP AI Core with  BYOM-LLM models hosted in private datacenter ?

 

Regards,

B.Srinath

could anyone help me? 
thank you

entry.title: Re: Multilingual content in SuccessFactors Learning

Dave, please see two screenshots of how to access the location for the settings. Please let me know if you are still stuck. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Increase in Non Completions for online Learning items...
entry.content:
Andy, 

This requires some amount of troubleshooting. A question that would help us in addressing the issue:

Question: Is the issue of non-completions specific to some users? (i.e. other users are completing the same item with no issue)

If No, it's happening to everyone, then can you send us some screenshots of the Item > Online settings tab?

If yes, then it's likely something to do with the browser and/or environment settings. It needs some troubleshooting over webinar. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Sci-Kit learn library in SAP Datasphere Data Flow
entry.content:

Many thanks,

Moritz

entry.title: Re: Upload External event learning history in bulk.
entry.content:
Hi Gi, I am pasting the link below to the APIs. However, I would not recommend this approach. The traditional method I have recommended is to use an Item, and mark completion against that item using a Learning History Connector. You can create both the item, and the learning history using Item Connector and Learning History Connector respectively. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_LEARNING/5aab9bef78fc4c4fa199c1f7aa142720/1da023d82c524aae853bca590b2f9ed0.html

entry.title: Re: DM Core Plugin Extension - how to avoid deploying all...

Hope i could explain it.

By the way I am using Google Cloud.

BR.

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion in People Story reports

entry.title: Re: sap cpi Set Timer issue
entry.content:
Hello user,

You can schedule the same with advanced timer configuration. The below schedule will run from morning 6:30 to 12:00 PM as we cannot stop at 11:40 as the minutes will be configured for every 30th minute

entry.title: Re: Bulk Upload Items in LMS
entry.content:
Hamza, 

For 400 courses, you have two options:

1) The traditional method is to use something called an Item Connector. The link is below. If you need assistance, please let me know. We provide SAP partner consulting services as it can get messy if you haven't use it before.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_LEARNING/360d9b368c7b41c3aede8505d0f3bd56/9aa1cc29a39e46e897d7daf43e2b7a70.html?q=item+connector

2) In case it's a one-off situation and you don't plan to create another batch of 400 items, you are better off handling it manually spread over a couple of days. Use the "Copy Item" option to speed up your process. 

Thank you,

entry.title: Display of current material stock in FSM
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are currently in discussion with a customer regarding the mentioned topic and are looking for a smart solution.

We are using an integrated scenario S/4HANA onPrem with FSM. A technician consumes an unplanned material while executing an activity on his mobile device. The related confirmation is sent back via the FSM web applicationto the backend system. After the confirmation of the unplanned material consumption in the backend, the stock is updated for the material and then the update is also replicated to FSM and to the mobile client.

This means that in the period between the material consumption on the mobile client and the confirmation in the backend, the displayed material stock in FSM does not reflect the reality. Is there any possibility to show the “real” available stock? For example, software from other manufacturers distinguishes between the system stock (from the backend) and the available stock (stock in backend minus recorded material consumptions).

Is there anybody who has an idea to solve this issue?

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,

Christoph

entry.title: Expired Tablets? Not on our Watch!Linking and Tracing value KPI to process PPI to DQ/Analytic/AI KPI
entry.content:
Background

In the consumer healthcare world, trust is paramount. Consumers rely on us for products that deliver on their promises, and safety is non-negotiable. But behind every bottle of cough syrup or pack of pain relievers lies a complex web of processes that must work flawlessly.

Behind every bottle of pills lies a data-driven secret to winning market share. In this blog, Let us discover how optimizing batch and shelf-life processes, guided by the right KPIs, can unlock greater efficiency and consumer trust.

 Why this process is critical to the consumer health care?

Effective batch management and shelf-life tracking are essential for consumer healthcare companies, leading to:

  • Increased Product Availability: Ensures products are consistently in stock, both in stores and online, leading to higher purchase frequency and household penetration.
  • Enhanced Consumer Trust: Demonstrates a commitment to quality and safety, building brand loyalty and encouraging consumers to explore more product categories as there are less product recalls.
  • Optimized Distribution: Minimizes waste by prioritizing the distribution of products with shorter shelf lives, ensuring fresher products reach consumers.
  • Improved Consumer Experience: Reduces the risk of consumers receiving expired or ineffective products, leading to higher satisfaction, repeat purchases, and increased household penetration.
  • Stronger E-commerce Performance: Ensures that products sold online have valid shelf lives and arrive in optimal condition, boosting consumer trust and driving online sales.
  • Enhanced Brand Reputation & Compliance: Strengthens the brand's reputation for safety and reliability by meeting regulatory standards, attracting and retaining customers.

What Business Value and Process and Analytic KPI's need to be set up for driving process efficiencies?

Ditch the spreadsheets and gut feelings! To build a truly efficient consumer healthcare supply chain, you need KPIs that pack a punch.Tired of expired products eating into your profits? The cure is clear: KPIs that connect the dots between business value, process optimization, and data-driven insights. Get ready to transform your batch management and shelf-life tracking from cost center to competitive edge.

Business Value KPI: 

Product Market Volume or Share can be used to measure the business value KPI i.e increase in number of products sold and revenue share.

Process Efficiency KPI(s):

To measure the process effectiveness of batch management and shelf life tracking, you can use the following five KPIs (Key Performance Indicators):

  • Batch Accuracy Measures the percentage of batches that are correctly recorded and matched with the correct product, production date, and shelf life information. This helps ensure compliance and reduces errors.
  • Shelf Life Compliance Rate Tracks the percentage of products that are used or shipped before their expiration date. It indicates how well the organization is managing product rotations and preventing the use of expired stock.
  • Batch Traceability Rate Measures the percentage of batches that can be fully traced back to raw materials, production processes, and final products. High traceability is critical for quality control, recalls, and regulatory compliance.
  • Inventory Turnover by Batch Indicates how often inventory (organized by batch) is sold or used in a given time period. A high turnover rate suggests efficient batch management, preventing stock from aging or becoming obsolete.
  • Expired/Out-of-Spec Product Percentage Tracks the percentage of products that are expired, approaching expiration, or outside of defined quality standards. A low percentage indicates effective shelf life tracking and proactive batch management.

Analytic KPI's

You're tracking batch numbers and expiry dates, but are you truly analyzing your shelf-life performance? To move beyond the basics, here are 3 KPIs that reveal hidden insights, optimize inventory, and protect your bottom line.

  • Shelf-Life Consumption Rate by Channel: This KPI goes beyond the average consumption rate and breaks it down by distribution channel (e.g., online, pharmacy, supermarket). This helps identify channels where products are moving slower and might be at higher risk of expiry, allowing for targeted inventory adjustments.
  • Days to Expiry at Time of Sale: This measures how close products are to their expiration date when they are sold. Tracking this metric over time and by product/batch can reveal potential issues in your supply chain or distribution network. For example, consistently high numbers might indicate a need to improve stock rotation practices or adjust delivery routes.
  • Write-Off Cost per Batch: This KPI directly links shelf-life management to financial performance. By analyzing the cost of writing off expired products on a per-batch basis, you can pinpoint problematic products, batches, or even production runs that consistently result in higher losses. This data can then be used to investigate root causes, optimize production processes, or adjust forecasting models.

Data Quality KPI's:

Data holds the key to eliminating expired products from your supply chain. By tracking these 3 DQ KPIs, consumer healthcare companies can make smarter decisions, reduce waste, and boost profitability.

Data Completeness Rate: This KPI tracks the percentage of batch records that contain all required fields (e.g., batch number, production date, expiration date, storage conditions).

  • Formula: (Number of Complete Batch Records / Total Batch Records) * 100
  • Goal: A higher completeness rate ensures that accurate, full data is available for decision-making and reduces errors related to missing information that could lead to expired products being shipped.

Data Accuracy Rate :Measures the accuracy of data entered into the system (e.g., correct batch number, correct expiration date, accurate shelf life data). Ensuring data is error-free is key for reliable batch management and expiration tracking.

  • Formula: (Correctly Entered Data / Total Data Entries) * 100

Goal: A high accuracy rate minimizes costly errors in inventory management and ensures shelf life data is reliable for decision-making.


Real-time Data Availability (Latency) : Tracks how quickly batch data (including shelf life and expiration dates) is updated and available for use across systems in real time.

  • Formula: Average Time for Data Update from Entry to Availability
  • Goal: Minimize latency to ensure real-time decisions are made, especially for perishable goods, ensuring that expired products are flagged before distribution.

Batch Analytics Utilization Rate : The percentage of batch-related decisions that rely on advanced analytics, such as predictive analytics for expiration forecasts or automated shelf life recommendations.

  • Formula: (Number of Analytics-driven Decisions / Total Decisions) * 100
  • Goal: Increase the use of data analytics for batch forecasting, enabling better stock management, reduced waste, and more efficient shelf life tracking.

AI KPI's

AI isn't magic, it's measurable. Track these KPIs to ensure your AI investments are delivering tangible results.

  • Shelf-Life Prediction Error: Measures the difference between the AI's predicted shelf life and the actual shelf life of a batch. A lower error rate indicates a more accurate and reliable AI model.
  • Demand Forecast Accuracy: Tracks how well the AI predicts future demand for products, considering factors like seasonality, promotions, and external trends. Higher accuracy leads to better production planning and reduced waste.
  • Automated Decision Rate: Measures the percentage of batch-related decisions (e.g., stock allocation, replenishment orders) made automatically by the AI system. A higher rate indicates increased efficiency and reduced manual effort.
  • False Positive Rate (for Expiry Alerts): Tracks how often the AI incorrectly flags products as nearing expiry. A low false-positive rate is crucial to avoid unnecessary interventions and maintain trust in the system.

How we can achieve the process efficiency?

To achieve the efficiency in batch management and shelf life tracking within an SAP environment, several SAP data objects need to be cleansed and maintained properly. Clean data ensures accurate reporting, effective analytics, and improved decision-making. Below are the key SAP data objects that require cleansing:

1. Material Master (MM)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Material Number: Ensure no duplicate or incorrect material numbers are present.
  • Basic Data: Verify the accuracy of material descriptions, units of measure, and classification.
  • Batch Management Indicator: This flag determines whether batch management is activated for a material. Shelf Life Data: Ensure expiration dates, shelf life period, and "Best Before" information are consistent and up to date.
  • Why it's Important: The material master drives many processes in batch management, including the correct tracking of shelf life and batch-specific information.

2. Batch Master (BM) / Batch Management Data

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Batch Number: Ensure that batch numbers are unique, and the proper batch number ranges are defined.
  • Batch Status: Check that the status of the batch is accurate (e.g., whether it’s blocked, released, or expired).
  • Batch-Specific Attributes: Data fields related to production date, shelf life, and expiration dates need to be consistently maintained.
  • Why it's Important: Batch data is directly tied to shelf life management, product traceability, and ensuring that expired products are flagged in the system.

3. Vendor Master (VM)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Vendor Data: Cleanse and verify that supplier details are correctly entered, including lead times and supply conditions.
  • Contract Data: Ensure consistency in contracts related to batch management, particularly with suppliers offering products with specific shelf life requirements.
  • Why it's Important: Proper vendor data ensures that the raw materials and products you receive have the expected shelf life, leading to better batch and expiration tracking.

4. Inventory Management Data (IM)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Stock Levels: Verify that stock quantities are accurate and up-to-date.
  • Batch Assignment: Ensure that inventory is correctly linked to the right batch and that batch information (production date, expiry) is accurate.
  • Why it's Important: Accurate stock and batch-level data are crucial for ensuring that inventory management processes do not allow expired batches to be used or sold.

5. Quality Management (QM) Data

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Inspection Lots: Make sure that all inspection lots are correctly assigned to batches and include expiration or shelf life information.
  • Test Results: Ensure that any quality tests performed on batches reflect true shelf life conditions and are properly recorded.
  • Defect Codes and Alerts: Cleanse the defect categories to ensure accurate reporting on product quality, particularly when related to expiration issues.
  • Why it's Important: Ensures the quality of batches is properly tracked and that expired or defective goods are identified before shipment.

6. Production Planning and Control (PP)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Production Order Data: Ensure that production orders contain accurate batch numbers, manufacturing dates, and expected shelf lives.
  • Routing and Bill of Materials (BOM): Clean and maintain up-to-date data on materials used in batch production, along with their shelf life parameters.
  • Why it's Important: Production orders must align with batch management and shelf life tracking to ensure product quality and timely usage.

7. Sales and Distribution (SD)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Sales Order Data: Ensure sales orders are correctly linked to the right batches and product shelf life. Shipping Data: Verify that shipping information includes accurate shelf life and batch data to prevent the shipment of expired products.
  • Why it's Important: Ensures that products are shipped in compliance with their expiration and shelf life constraints.

8. Warehouse Management (WM)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Storage Location Data: Ensure proper categorization of products in different storage locations based on batch and shelf life. Movement Types and Data: Cleanse movement data related to batch transfers and adjustments.
  • Why it's Important: Warehouse data ensures that products are stored in compliance with their required shelf life conditions, and that batches are rotated correctly.

9. Change Management Data (in SAP GRC)

  • Key Fields to Cleanse: Change Logs: Ensure accurate tracking of changes made to material and batch master data, particularly when shelf life or batch attributes are adjusted.
  • Why it's Important: Enables traceability and compliance with audit requirements, especially when shelf life or batch details are modified.

By cleansing these key SAP data objects, you'll ensure accurate tracking of batches, shelf life, and expiration dates, which are critical for improving inventory management, reducing waste, and ensuring compliance.

Data without impact is just noise. How are YOU turning data initiatives into tangible business value? Let's compare notes!

entry.title: Re: b1if, use of table name and table field name in payload
entry.content:
Hi,

you have to switch the retrival mode. You can find it under Step Design -> Inbound -> Retrieval -> Retrieval Mode. I think "All with Public Properties (NodesAsProperties)" shows the properties and "All with Field Names (AllNodes)" shows the tablenames, but I'm not sure. You have to try it out.

entry.title: Re: Internal Process Create and Confirm Adhoc HU WT using RFUI
entry.content:
I have an additional step in which RFUI asks me to confirm destination HU / bin.

Any way to skip this?

entry.title: ABAP CDS View - Length of CAST rounded in 7.40 SP8
entry.content:
I have an issue with the CAST in this CDS:

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ztest1'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.CompareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Test'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view ztest as select from ztable
{
    key case when input2 = 'RACINE_INCLUSION' then 'I' 
             when input2 = 'RACINE_EXCLUSION' then 'E'
             else '' end as sign,
    key 'CP' as option_,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Like FIPEX data element'
    cast( output1 as abap.char(24)) as low,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Like FIPEX data element'
    cast( '' as abap.char(24)) as high
}
where input1 = 'NATPCE_EMPR'

 

In both CDS information and in ABAP debug, LOW and HIGH are 30 characters instead of 24:

For information, ZTABLE-OUTPUT1 is length 50.

I tested other lengths which are rounded from 9 (1 to 8, 10, 30, 50, 100 not rounded):

  • 9 is rounded to 10
  • 11 to 29: rounded to 30
  • 31 to 49: rounded to 50
  • 51 to 99: rounded to 100
  • Others are not tested

No reason found in the documentation and in the SAP notes.

Do you have any idea why the length is rounded? Any official link? Is it only 7.40 SP8 and solved after?

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: for information, the ABAP SELECT CAST equivalence is fine, RANGE-LOW and HIGH are 24 characters long:

    SELECT CASE WHEN input2 = 'RACINE_INCLUSION' THEN 'I'
                WHEN input2 = 'RACINE_EXCLUSION' THEN 'E'
                ELSE ' ' END                              AS sign,
           'CP'                                           AS option,
           CAST( output1 AS CHAR( 24 ) )                  AS low,
           CAST( ' ' AS CHAR( 24 ) )                      AS high
      FROM ztable
      WHERE input1 = 'NATPCE_EMPR'
      INTO TABLE @DATA(range) ##NEEDED.

Software and versions:

  • ABAP: 7.40 SP8
  • EHP7 FOR SAP ERP 6.0
  • Kernel: 753 SP1300
  • Database: Oracle 19.14.0.0.0

entry.title: Re: An Abap REST Client
entry.content:
hello everyone,

@JoeGoerlich thanks, but I have a doubt about what we should do regarding the note, stop using the SA38? or the RSICFCLTST01 report?

entry.title: What is SAP RISE support model
entry.content:
Just as there are misconceptions about SAP's offering RISE with SAP, the SAP RISE support model also appears to be misunderstood. I am writing this article to clarify and provide a better understanding.

The SAP RISE support model is a comprehensive framework designed to assist organizations in transitioning to the cloud and adopting SAP's Intelligent Enterprise framework. It offers a standardized end-to-end methodology, including onboarding sessions, a dedicated launch advisor, ..... to ensure a smooth migration with minimal risks and disruptions.

By providing a structured approach, SAP RISE enables businesses to innovate, optimize processes, and scale operations efficiently, leveraging advanced analytics, artificial intelligence, and automation technologies to remain competitive in dynamic markets.

Key Components of the SAP RISE Support Model:

  1. Guided Methodology

    • A structured approach that includes planning, execution, and optimization phases to ensure a smooth migration to the cloud.
    • Incorporates SAP’s best practices to align with business objectives and digital transformation goals.

  2. Onboarding Sessions

    • Dedicated sessions to familiarize customers with SAP RISE, its components, and capabilities.
    • Helps establish clear migration roadmaps and ensures all stakeholders are aligned on expectations and milestones.

  3. Launch Advisor

    • A dedicated advisor to guide businesses through their initial phases of cloud migration and adoption.
    • Provides expert advice to mitigate risks, address challenges, and ensure timely execution.

  4. Project Management Tools

    • Advanced tools like SAP Cloud ALM to track project progress, manage tasks, and monitor risks.
    • Facilitates collaboration among SAP, implementation partners, and customer teams for streamlined project execution.
    • Ensures visibility and control over project deliverables and timelines.

  5. Quality Gates Support

    • Predefined checkpoints (quality gates) at key stages of the migration process.
    • Validates that all deliverables meet required standards before moving to the next phase.
    • Helps identify and address potential risks early, ensuring adherence to quality and minimizing delays.

  6. Comprehensive Support Services

    • Incident management, troubleshooting, and advisory services through SAP Enterprise Support or SAP Preferred Success.
    • Continuous performance monitoring and proactive optimization to prevent issues and improve efficiency.

  7. Business Process Intelligence (BPI)

    • Analytical tools to map, analyze, and optimize business processes.
    • Identifies inefficiencies and areas for improvement before and after migration to ensure optimal performance.

  8. Cloud Infrastructure and Operations

    • Provides managed cloud services across SAP’s private cloud or hyperscalers like AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud.
    • Ensures scalability, cost optimization, and robust infrastructure management.

  9. Security and Compliance Assistance

    • Expert guidance to ensure data security, regulatory compliance, and adherence to global standards.
    • Provides regular updates and patches to maintain a secure and compliant environment.

  10. Outcome-Based SLAs

    • Service-level agreements focused on achieving business outcomes, such as system availability, performance, and operational efficiency.

  11. Continuous Improvement

    • Post-go-live support for updates, innovation, and process optimization to ensure ongoing success and alignment with evolving business needs.

This comprehensive support model ensures businesses can transition to the cloud efficiently, with a focus on achieving operational excellence and long-term value from their digital transformation efforts.

I am not 100% sure if everyone gets CSP (Customer Success Partner) to guide the customer through onboarding, migration, etc, along with launch advisor so I did not include it in Comprehensive Support Services

As SAP refines the model, certain aspects may change or might have already evolved. If I’ve made any errors, please let me know. I aimed to present this in a simple and easy-to-understand manner, even for those new to the topic. If you have any suggestions or corrections, feel free to message me privately, and I’ll gladly review and update it as needed.

entry.title: Re: about vulnerability CVE-2025-0066 in SAP note 3550708
entry.content: Thanks you Sandra

entry.title: Consignment Settlement Report consider Batch Price
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know how consignment settlement report consider the price.

There is active Info Record and Batch price is also maintained. Consignment settlement report consider the batch price.
What changes we need to do if we need the PIR price in settlement report?

entry.title: Re: Error in VIM Workfllow - Container element Type conflict
entry.content: How do you solved it? I have the same issue!!

entry.title: Re: Total Target Training Hours Requirement
entry.content:
Gi, 

This might be a tad late a question to ask - typically should be done at the time of the original implementation. But it's never too late to course correct, so here is what I would recommend going forward.

1. Ensure you go from 3 systems to 2 systems: SAP and Percipio are both formal systems, and so reporting is easier, if the system is fed the right information.   I will speak for the SAP/SF LMS - each time you load external history, since the duration (or credit) is an important field, do NOT allow your end users to load history by themselves, at least not yet. I would recommend a 2 stage approach below:

STAGE 1 (for at least a few months): Have a simple email template they need to fill out when they send you the information about their external training. It would include fields you need very importantly, such as (a) Course Title (b) Training Vendor name (c) Duration and/or Credit hours. Ideally you should create a reusable item for each of these courses, clearly identify a training vendor name, and then mark completion against that item. Some of my customers have created just 1 item for all external training, something I don't prefer because running reports becomes difficult, but that's an option too.

STAGE 2: I have seen customers over the years who have created items for external courses, and they allow end-users to mark completion against only those items. It needs some fine-tuning because it all depends on whether you have a good pool of reusable items (e.g. every year, a lot of users take the same training, so why not have 1 item for that training and everyone that goes to the training can mark themselves complete for that training). 

Hope the ideas above help, but it's a tall order for you since the system(s) are already working and change can be difficult. Let me know if you need more help/suggestions. Thank you,

entry.title: Re: External Event - required fields
entry.content:
Hi Gi, 

Not feasible to do it. In the database, Total Hours and Credit Horus are not primary keys. Please see my separate response to your other post - you should NOT have endusers mark completions themselves, it's a mess. 

Thank you,

entry.title: after create a new banner, the old title in the text area is still displayed after saving
entry.content:
Hello

When I create a new banner in Module Learning Management (Success Factors) and enter a new title in the text area, the old title is still displayed on the banner after saving. We want to show the new learning catalog in 2025. The new titel is not being updated. The only way, that we found, is to delete the cache. But it's not a good and easy way let our ca. 5000 users delete the browser cache.

Any idea, how can I configure it?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Standard Audit File-Tax (SAF-T) - Statutory Reporting in Poland (JPK) - SAP Product Strategy
entry.content:
In this webinar (Standard Audit File-Tax (SAF-T) - Statutory Reporting in Poland (JPK) - SAP Product Strategy) here at the end of the last year there was an item on the agenda called "'Transition from SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements’ to ‘SAP Document and Reporting Compliance’".
We are thinking of implementing SAP and are therefor looking for a solution for our Polish subsidiary to cover the requirements of the Polish tax authorities for mandatory SAF-T reporting. As we plan to use SAP S/4HANA public cloud, the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements can not be used (as far as I understood).
In 2023 ‘SAP Document and Reporting Compliance’ was not yet able to cover the requirements of the Polish authorities. But what about today?

entry.title: Re: after create a new banner, the old title in the text...
entry.content:
Tamiri, 

I have seen the issue and you are right about cache - it does go away, is it urgent (i.e. within 24 hours or less) that everyone should see the new banner? If not, then just wait for a day and the network cache should update the banner for everyone automatically. 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: after create a new banner, the old title in the text...
entry.content: Hi PLESM,

entry.title: Re: after create a new banner, the old title in the text...
entry.content: thank you for your answer. Already everyone can see the new banner because we made an announcement in our intranet. But the problem ist the old titel, that is still desplayed after many times of saving.

entry.title: HCM Brasil - 13º Salário Maternidade Adoção - Pagamento efetuado pelo INSS
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

No dia 3 de janeiro de 2025 foram entregues duas SAP Note referentes ao envio para o eSocial dos valores de 13º salário maternidade adoção que foram pagos pelo INSS.

Com essa solução, temos uma nova rubrica de caráter informativa (/362 - 13º lic.mat. INSS eSocial) que contêm especificamente a parte paga pelo INSS dos valores de 13º salário maternidade adoção para o reporte ao eSocial.

Nesta entrega as seguintes SAP Notes foram liberadas:

  • 3483047 - Maternity leave 13th salary - Payment by INSS.
  • 3483598 - eSocial - S-1010 - Maternity adoption 13th salary - Payment by INSS.

Importante

Essa nova rubrica tem seu valor baseado na rubrica /349 (13º Licença Matern.), seu valor é acumulado na /142 (Base FGTS (13o)) e é gerada apenas nos cálculos de folha de 1313 e 13DI.

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você consegue encontrar na SAP Note 3556989 - eSocial - Overview - 2025.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias.

Um abraço,

Anderson Bueno

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@ncktz Picking up your point:

How will this work with the new Authorization Management Service that SAP "generally recommends for newly built applications" (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/authorization-management-service-in-sap-cloud-i...) and that requires users to be replicated to the Identity Directory Service? 
I guess this is also what @H_Ettelbrueck mentioned with "especially looking for the "deeper" integration which also populates SAP Cloud Identity's identity directory, in line with our strategic direction."
Is this already planned to be released in the near future or when is this expected to work? Currently, it seems to me like two approaches that unfortunately do not fit together (yet?).

I assume your point is: This blog "only" describes the federation approach, where users and their assignments are only stored in Entra ID, and IAS acts as pure proxy, propagating the information received from Entra ID to BTP. This works for "traditional" BTP applications that use the SAP Authorization and Trust Management service (XSUAA) which supports this federation approach, and not for newer applications which natively use SAP Cloud Identity service and specifically its Authorization Management service. That's because the latter requires users to exist in the Identity Directory of Cloud Identity, and assignments being stored there, too.

My answer is twofold then:

  • The federation approach is also planned for the Authorization Management service, since heading for feature parity (+ some advantages, of course). See the following roadmap entry: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73555000100800000287&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q1%202025;INNO=15836D3743D01EDF8EBAF3E508C9F581
  • Strategically, I definitely recommend to still populate the Identity Directory with all relevant users, because there's a growing amount of applications and scenarios that require this data to be available for different reasons. In general, our vision for SAP Cloud Identity services is to become a central integration hub for IAM between your corporate IAM and the whole SAP cloud landscape, and handle the integration between Cloud Identity and the applications more and more by SAP ("SAP-managed" setup). That means, it's not about establishing yet another target for your user provisioning, but to reduce the whole number you have today by ideally just a single one, namely your Cloud Identity tenant.

@MartinRaepple Seems like we should also describe this "provisioning" case, replicating original users, groups, and assignments, from Entra ID to the Identity Directory 😉

entry.title: BEx Callback to AfO Callback migration
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm currently dealing with a very old BEx workbook that has some VBA code in it. There is a Sub 

 

Public CallBack(ParamArray varname())
  'Currently this callback routine is only called for Grid Items
  'varname(0): BExExcelItem.DataProvider.Name
  'varname(1): BExExcelItem.Range
  'varname(2): BExExcelItem.Name
......
End Sub

 

that has been registered in the BEx Menu to be called after variables refresh.

Now I need to get this working with AfO... I figured out to register the callback as described here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_ANALYSIS_OFFICE/ca9c58444d64420d99d6c136a3207632/f26f56246c9b1014bf2c9a7eb0e91070.html?locale=en-US&q=DataSources

However I can't access varname() anymore. Is there some kind of equivalent to this?

entry.title: Re: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM - Transfer data from Billable Items to FI documents
entry.content:
Hi @MariusTack ,

We are also trying to achieve the same functionality but while adding the custom field in Business Context 'CINV_BILLABLEITEMS_MAIN' - under that below business scenarios are not available

 Contract Accounting Business Partner Items
 Contract Accounting Total Record
 Accounting Journal Entry Item

So we are not able to enable the custom field for those scenarios. Is there a way where we can add these scenarios under context ' Convergent Invoicing: Billable Items (CINV_BILLABLEITEMS_MAIN)'?

 

When I try to add the field with same name in different Business Context 'FICA_DOCUMENT_BP_ITEM', it is showing error as same name cannot be used. Can you please help how it was added in two different Business Context?

entry.title: Re: About Myself

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to share a link to a library without S...
entry.content:
Emelie, 

I'll make the assumption that the external user in this case has SAP IAS access. If yes, then when you generate the Direct Link, set the "Learning Only Users" to Yes (refer attached screenshot). 

 

In the event the user does NOT yet have an SAP IAS account, you can use "Sites" functionality. Let me know if you need to go into that. 

Thank you, 

Sumanth

entry.title: Re: after create a new banner, the old title in the text...
entry.content: Tamiri, the old title is probably going to autocorrect in 24 hours. If it doesn't, please post a screenshot. Sorry it's network cache and we are mostly LMS experts here, and our suggestions/advice is at best based on our past experiences. Rest assured (based on our experience) your issue should resolve itself in the next 24 hours.

entry.title: Re: Final settlement still credit customer after accrual...
entry.content:
What you describe is standard behavior. The settlement amount can be equal, larger or smaller than the accrual. Condition type REU1 is setting the accrual amount to zero, but it is not influencing the settlement amount.

Within the standard customizing scales are used for setting the target / rebate threshold. If done right this ensures that a settlement amount of zero is calculated if the target / rebate threshold is not met. 

Based on your description I assume that in your case somebody decides manually if the target / rebate threshold is met. Setting REU1 will not impact the settlement amount. Instead you need a 100% discount condition that is part of the pricing procedures for accrual and settlement.

entry.title: can't open this link - replicate SQLA to msSQL
entry.content:
I can also provide some further technical information about synchronizing to Microsoft SQL Server:

  • The general tutorial for working with MobiLink in Sybase Central and with a MobiLink Synchronization Model is here:

    http://dcx.sap.com/index.html#sa160/en/mlstart/sqlatut.html

    Initially, you may want to follow it "as-is" (which uses a SQL Anywhere consolidated database) in order to get a better understanding of the MobiLink product. You can then move on to using a Microsoft SQL Server consolidated database afterwards.

  • Changing the tutorial to work with a different consolidated database is mostly related to creating an ODBC DSN that targets a Microsoft SQL Server instance. You would pick that DSN when defining a consolidated database in your MobiLink synchronization model. The rest of the instructions regarding creating scripts and models is the same, but using Microsoft SQL Server SQL syntax (T-SQL) instead of SQL Anywhere syntax inside the synchronization scripts.

  • You will need to install the MobiLink system tables for Microsoft SQL Server. The script to do this can be found at: %SQLANY16%\MobiLink\setup\syncmss.sql. If you are using Sybase Central to create a Synchronization Model though, once you connect to Microsoft SQL Server, you will be prompted to install the MobiLink system tables automatically (i.e. run the script).

  • There are some Microsoft SQL Server specific issues that you will want to be aware of:
    http://dcx.sap.com/index.html#sa160/en/mlserver/ml-basics-mss-5159304.html

I have contacted some people internally here at SAP about consulting engagements to see if they can assist. If you are already working with an SAP Sales representative, it would probably be best to inquire with them directly and look into your available options.

entry.title: Re: MessageTransformBean module parameter for header line in SFTP Receiver adapter
SAp Enable Now Producer Connection error
entry.content:
Hello 

I'm trying to open Producer this morning, it gives me a connection error.  Pic attached. 

What could be the problem.  My customer is having issues also, but about a Microset. saw the error message very quicly 

I have re downloaded the App launcher, first repair, then - removed and re installed again,  Still same message 

Any ideas ? 

Thank you

entry.title: CPI - if function returns multiple values
entry.content:
the if function is returning always the true value and the false value together in the same context, which makes my target structure take the false value (as being the first value).

I could manage the issue with the following udf-script and mapping:

def void conditionalMappingSingle(String[] conditions, String[] trueValues, String[] falseValues, Output output, MappingContext context) {
    // Loop through the conditions and find the first true value
    for (int i = 0; i < conditions.length; i++) {
        if ("true".equalsIgnoreCase(conditions[i])) {
            output.addValue(trueValues[i]); // Add the corresponding true value to the output
            return; // Exit after finding the first true value
        }
    }
    // If no true value is found, add the first false value to the output
    if (falseValues.length > 0) {
        output.addValue(falseValues[0]); // Default to the first false value
    }
}

Using if (standard function)

 

 

using user-defined function:

 

Isn't there a way to solve it with the standard mapping functions?

entry.title: Executive Review 'look & feel'
entry.content:
Hello

I am looking for some advice please regarding the look of the Executive Review display screen.

When viewing Executive Review, primarily on a laptop screen, there is hardly any space for the rows of user data. You can usually only see 3 or 4 rows. I know there is an expand arrow on the top right, but this doesn't really do anything (expands to just 5 or 6 rows).

Just wondered if others are experiencing this too, and if anyone has any tips on how best to view the Executive Review screen (without having to massively zoom out!).

Thanks, Sam

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Hi @rupamaity 

I'm trying to understand your statement about holistic view:

SAP Build contains all those other products, but SAP Build Code is just an IDE (BAS) with the addition of Joule and some guidelines. Is that correct?

entry.title: Re: GRIR unable to clear due to Reference Key 3 not diffe...
entry.content: try to untick special treatment account GR/IR in selection screen

entry.title: Re: MessageTransformBean module parameter for header line...
entry.content:
To add header line with all field names you can simply add one field with sample name like "datafields" and map the field with constant. In constant you can give all the field names along with static field separately.

datafields -> mapped with constant -> field1,field2,field3 ( for comma separated field separator)

 

This works in my case as the first field will be represented as all field names followed by your actual fields

entry.title: Merge two cubes based on two common references?
entry.content: I want to start with my orders database and merge another query related to shipments. Sometimes the common field between the two queries is one reference and sometimes it is another. It is possible to create a cube that pulls the shipment data based on the match from the first reference, then shipment data based on the match from the second reference if the first is null or no match?

entry.title: Re: Archived MDG Change Request isn't listed in "Display...
entry.content: I see that there is no read program assigned for USMD_CR in AOBJ. Is that the reason not able to see the archived CR when we enable the check box "Include Archived change requests"?.

entry.title: Goods issue in S/4 without using EWM for delivery note printout
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S/4 2022. I just want to confirm.

Delivery note cannot be printed without doing a goods issue in outbound delivery? is my assumption correct?

entry.title: Touch points in SD when creating Sales order with TM
entry.content:
Hi All,

What are the integrations or touch points in OTC flow if we are using S4 HANA without using EWM but only TM.

- First touch point is when we create a Sales order , here we get freight unit from TM.

- Any other touch points from TM once planning is completed for example once Freight order is created?

entry.title: Sold subscription product on invoice issued from S4hana ublic cloud
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Since last year, the ID and description of the subscription product has been removed from the invoice form, so now only the ID and description of the rate element is printed.

When I look at the field mapping of the standard integration flow for the Transfer of Billing Data to Sales Billing (https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/integration/transfer-of-billing-data-to-sales-billing) I see that info "SoldProduct" is mapped and transferred to the billing document request.

Do you know if it can be found in any CDS view?
For example, BillingPerformancePeriodStrDte and BillingPerformancePeriodEndDte are also not part of the standard output form template but they can be accessed via CDS view I_BillingDocumentItemBasic but here the rate element ID is considered the "product number".

Thank you in advance for your input.

entry.title: Re: Goods issue in S/4 without using EWM for delivery note printout
entry.content: Why would it be printed before goods issue?

entry.title: Re: (UI5) Web Components enablement for OpenUI5/SAPUI5
entry.content:
in the documentation for the controls wrapping the components now there's the following message

"Deprecated as of version 1.120. this Web Components consumption model has been discarded. Until an alternative Web Components consumption model is available in the future, use sap.m and sap.f controls with similar functionality."
 
wonder what will the new model will be 🤔

entry.title: Re: CPI - if function returns multiple values
entry.content: The screenshots are tiny.
